comments.body: Thanks for the early review here!  I think this PR should have been marked as draft - this is just a work in progress for now.
LGTM, but definitely want other eyeballs on this one.
I'd also retitle the PR to "proxy: Add LayerInfoJSON API" so that it shows up more nicely in a merge commit too.

> * Do we want a new test of some kind? Or would this just rely on the `ostree-rs-ext` tests in #1808?

@cgwalters WDYT?

I'm ok just relying on the reverse dependency tests for now...longer term, one thing I've been thinking we could do perhaps is implement `skopeo copy --internal-via-proxy` that would actually cause skopeo to fork itself and copy via the proxy code.  That would allow us to gain a whole lot of testing for free.  (To fully generalize it we'd need write support in the proxy too, but copying from anything to an `oci` directory should be easy)

(Having a “proxy client” transport in c/image is potentially interesting. For a time, long ago, someone was repeatedly asking for a “copy image over ssh” feature… but I haven’t heard about that for a long time now, I don’t know whether that’s because the feature is not actually needed or because those users were motivated to find another implementation.

I would recommend keeping both the server and client restricted to a “source”, at least for a first version: the source interface is fairly straightforward and doesn’t change that much, while the destination has has _much_ more reason to be extended or outright changed when looking for ways to speed up copies.)

Yeah, though the "over ssh" bit would require us to skip the local-only optimization of fd passing via pipe().  On that thread...I've been investigating Cap'n Proto more and thinking of using that for an "official" interface.

Also tangentially related to this is that I did float the idea at one point that skopeo could actually stop linking in the containers/storage code entirely and instead fork off `/usr/bin/podman` to be the backend for that, communicating via this protocol.  (The advantage of this would be a lot less code and fewer dependencies linked into the skopeo binary, fewer worries about version compat etc.)
